They’d been sketching in a notebook, trying to lose themselves in the lines of a fantastical creature, when Mark, from accounting, had sauntered in. Mark was a solid, unremarkable man, but he seemed to derive a peculiar satisfaction from making Alex’s life uncomfortable.

“Well, well, well,” he drawled, leaning against the counter, his arms crossed. “Look what we have here. Still rocking the… *ensemble*, I see.”

Alex didn’t look up, just continued shading the creature’s wing.
